title: RoQ-under-netem — iroh QUIC datagram congestion-control vs a media bitrate estimator (the C1 unknown)

harness.iroh: =1.0.0 (datagram path: conn.send_datagram / read_datagram / max_datagram_size / datagram_send_buffer_space; path RTT via conn.paths().rtt())
harness.source: synthetic CBR — 64 kbps, 20 ms frames (160 B payload/datagram, 50 fps), sequence + timestamp stamped. No codec/cpal (headless rule).
harness.topology: relay on node-1; roq-recv on node-2; roq-send on node-1. Media flows node-1 -> node-2 over the DIRECT (hole-punched, same-VPC) path.
harness.shaping: tc netem on node-1 ens5 egress, u32 dst-filtered to node-2 only (Mac SSH path unshaped); watchdog clears tc after 240-300s.

conditions.name: rate40-defaultbuf
conditions.note: default ~1 MiB send buffer barely touched; link/qdisc queue is the bottleneck

conditions.name: rate40-smallbuf
conditions.note: 8 KiB buffer fully consumed yet send_datagram still returns Ok — iroh DROPS OLDEST silently, never errors; RTT inflation identical

conditions.name: rate40-recvcheck
conditions.note: receiver got a CONTIGUOUS delayed prefix (seq 0..207, zero gaps) at ~link rate; the other ~70% never arrived in time. Receiver-side loss reads 0% while most of the stream is undelivered — proof that receiver loss alone is NOT a usable congestion signal under an over-cap source.

findings.datagram_primitive: CONFIRMED end-to-end over iroh 1.0 across the real NAT fabric: max_datagram_size 1162 B (ample for Opus 160 B frames); unreliable, unordered, no retransmit.
findings.under_loss_and_delay: iroh QUIC datagrams are TRANSPARENT to the media path — netem loss passes straight through as sequence gaps (4.6%~=5%, 30.9%~=30%), RTT stays flat (~2ms) and tracks added delay EXACTLY (102ms = 100 + base), jitter stays sub-ms. The estimator gets accurate loss + RTT + jitter. Audio holds to ~30% loss with visible (31% PLC) degradation, never silent — meets the design exit criterion.
findings.under_bandwidth_cap_below_source: iroh QUEUES and PACES datagrams IN ORDER rather than dropping to fit the link, so an over-cap source produces multi-second bufferbloat (RTT 537->8829ms) and the receiver sees an ever-delayed contiguous prefix at link rate (~11kbps). send_datagram returns Ok throughout (drops oldest silently when its own buffer fills); the datagram-send-buffer size changes only iroh's own queue contribution, not the link/qdisc queue, so RTT inflation is identical.
findings.C1_verdict: RESOLVED/characterized: the two controllers do NOT fight destructively, but iroh will NOT rate-adapt media for you. The media engine's estimator MUST be authoritative and MUST drive bitrate down off the path-RTT trend (+ per-stream sequence loss + arrival jitter) — exactly the design's C1 proposed solution point 2. E10 confirms all three signals (RTT, sequence-loss, jitter) are exposed and accurate, and that relying on send_datagram back-pressure OR receiver-side loss alone would fail.
